sanskrit: अथैतदप्यशक्तोऽसि कर्तुं मद्योगमाश्रितः । सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं ततः कुरु यतात्मवान् ॥
hindiTranslation: अगर मेरे योगसमता के आश्रित हुआ तू इसपूर्वश्लोकमें कहे गये साधन को भी करनेमें असमर्थ है, तो मनइन्द्रियोंको वशमें करके सम्पूर्ण कर्मोंके फलका त्याग कर ॥
englishTranslation: If you still cannot do even this while taking refuge in my yoga, then, self-controlled, renounce all fruits of action.
essence: Let go of the result; the action itself is the practice.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: If even devotion-filled practice feels too hard, give up the need to own the results of your actions. Stay controlled and let the work be enough.
simpleInsight: There is relief in releasing the demand that every action must pay you back.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen between duty and grief. Krishna has already offered steadier practices, and now lowers the threshold again: if even that is too hard, renounce attachment to the fruits of action and keep your mind controlled.
modernRelevance: You sent the message, then kept checking for a reply that could save or ruin your day. This verse asks you to stop feeding on the outcome and return to the act itself.
translationLiteral: अथ (then) / एतत् अपि (this also) / अशक्तः असि (you are unable) / कर्तुम् (to do) / मद्योगम् आश्रितः (taking refuge in my yoga) / सर्व-कर्म-फल-त्यागम् (renunciation of all fruits of action) / ततः (then) / कुरु (do) / यत-आत्मवान् (self-controlled)
insight: It shifts attention from owning results to releasing them after sincere effort.
